citation_short: Israel NII, Memorial Day casualty release, 2025
title: Since the beginning of the war until today, 934 civilians have been murdered
geographic_scope: Israel, with an Oct. 7 disaster breakout
date_scope: 2023-10-07 onward; breakout for the Oct. 7 disaster
strengths: Official Israeli civilian casualty source with an explicit Oct. 7 disaster subtotal.
limitations: Not disaggregated by attacked locality and published long after the episode.
notes: Used for the civilian direct-death numerator of 778.

title: Innovative Finance for Rebuilding Israel's Regions
strengths: Clear prewar resident count for the core Gaza-envelope area.
limitations: Regional aggregate rather than a complete attacked-locality-by-locality roll.
notes: Provides the core resident lower-bound block of 63,978 people.

title: Bank of Israel Annual Report 2023
strengths: Official bounded southern-sphere population that includes attacked cities such as Ofaqim and Netivot while staying subnational.
limitations: Broader than the directly attacked footprint, so it is used only as an upper scenario bound.
notes: The report states that 57 urban and rural localities in the southern sphere had about 150,000 residents at end-2022.

citation_short: Times of Israel IDF Oct. 7 probe summary, 2025
title: 767 troops faced 5,000 terrorists; Gaza Division was overrun for hours, IDF Oct. 7 probe finds
source_type: news report summarizing an IDF probe
pages_or_sections: article body; initial troop count and retake timeline
strengths: Most specific public account recovered for the initial IDF force posture and immediate retake chronology.
limitations: Media summary of a probe rather than the full underlying military appendix.
notes: Used for the 767-soldier floor and for bounding the retake window through 10 October.

citation_short: Times of Israel Nova probe summary, 2025
title: IDF okayed Nova music festival but didn't inform troops deployed at border, probe finds
source_type: news report summarizing an IDF probe
pages_or_sections: article body; attendance and security breakdown
geographic_scope: Nova festival site and adjacent Route 232 segment
strengths: Best recovered source for transient civilian concentration and for explicit police/IDF/Shin Bet handling at one major site.
limitations: Covers the Nova site and adjacent highway segment only, not all attacked roads and gatherings.
notes: Used conservatively to add 3,475 people (3,000 + 400 + 75) to the civilian lower bound.

citation_short: Times of Israel Sderot probe summary, 2025
title: 1,000 troops were in Sderot on Oct. 7, even as no further terrorists invaded city, probe finds
source_type: news report summarizing an IDF probe
pages_or_sections: article body; force concentration in Sderot
strengths: Demonstrates that same-day force density in a single attacked city could reach roughly 1,000 security personnel.
limitations: Mixed security category, not soldiers only, and city-specific rather than whole-theater.
notes: Used to justify that a post-767 attacked-footprint reinforcement scenario is plausible.

title: AP report on release of names of 310 soldiers killed in the initial Hamas attack and its aftermath
geographic_scope: Israel, initial Hamas attack and immediate aftermath
strengths: Provides a tight soldier-only fatality count tied to the initial attack and immediate aftermath.
limitations: Recovered at snippet level in this pass rather than from a structured casualty database.
notes: Used for the core soldier-only death numerator.

title: Authorities name 44 soldiers, 30 police officers killed in Hamas attack
pages_or_sections: article body; overview and Shin Bet count
strengths: Explicit public counts for police and Shin Bet fatalities and a warning that broader soldier rolls include local-security officers.
limitations: Rolling memorial article, not a formal denominator source.
notes: Used to keep police and Shin Bet explicit instead of silently merging them into the soldier-only DR.
